audit_items.proof_trace: Two `malloc` calls in `main()` have no NULL return-value checks: `dbuf.mv_data = malloc(dbuf.mv_size)` at L354 is dereferenced via `fgets(dbuf.mv_data, ...)` inside `readhdr()` at L73 if malloc fails; `kbuf.mv_data = malloc(kbuf.mv_size * 2)` at L384 is used immediately at L386 in pointer arithmetic `(char *)kbuf.mv_data + kbuf.mv_size`, which is undefined behaviour when the pointer is NULL. No intervening null check exists on either path.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker who can exhaust process memory before `mdb_load` is invoked (e.g., concurrent memory pressure on a low-memory host where mdb_load is used to import LDAP database content) causes `malloc` to return NULL; the subsequent null pointer dereference crashes the process (denial of service).
